claims.fieldOrBehavior: npm run validate:source -- passed (build succeeded silently, source-tree validation passed) on the #292 working tree.
npm run context-map -- --check reports docs/context-map.md is current.
bash evals/run.sh static: 31 PASS blocks / 0 FAIL blocks, exit 0.
bash evals/integration/test_stop_hook_release.sh: 20/20 assertions passed (scenarios A-H, AC1-AC7 covered), exit 0.
bash evals/integration/test_assignment_provider_local_file.sh: all checks passed (34 assertions incl. concurrent-claim race), exit 0 -- #290 back-compat holds.
bash evals/integration/test_assignment_provider_github.sh: all checks passed (45 assertions), exit 0 -- #290 back-compat holds; render-don't-execute contract unaffected.
bash evals/integration/test_liveness_heartbeat.sh: all checks passed, exit 0 -- liveness heartbeat/claim contract unaffected by the Stop-hook release path.

Non-manifest-command-backed summary (deliberately NOT recorded as test_output/command-backed per ADR 0020: npm run build, bare 'npm run validate:source' without the manifest's required trailing --, and test_trust_reconcile_manifest.sh are not verbatim run-baseline.sh --manifest-json entries). (1) npm run build --silent: exit 0, no output, build/ output regenerated. (2) npm run validate:source (bare, no trailing --): exit 0, 'Source tree validation passed' -- distinct from and in addition to the manifest-matched 'npm run validate:source --' check recorded separately above. (3) bash evals/integration/test_trust_reconcile_manifest.sh: exit 0, manifest entries=45, ci.yml required --check invocations=45, parity confirmed both directions. (4) Independent fail-open reproduction (AC7), NOT via the eval: built a corrupt (non-JSON) session-state sidecar file plus a valid-but-unresolved-actor fixture and invoked scripts/hooks/stop-goal-fit.js directly -- exit 0 both times, zero Node stack traces, exact expected stderr diagnostics ('actor identity is unresolved; never releasing under a synthetic identity' / clean silent bail on the corrupt sidecar), no liveness event emitted, the corrupt sidecar file left unchanged. Also reproduced the missing-build/ case via cp -r scripts/ into an isolated packageRoot with no build/ sibling (mirroring the eval's own F(i) technique independently): exit 2 (goal-fit's own pre-existing block-mode gate, unrelated to release logic), zero stack trace, exact 'build/src/cli/workflow-sidecar.js not available' diagnostic present. (5) Independent AC3 corroboration: grepped scripts/hooks/stop-goal-fit.js for spawnSync/execFileSync/exec( -- zero call sites reachable from releaseOnNonTerminalStop; reproduced a github-provider Stop event end-to-end against a fixture repo with a tripwire 'gh' shim prepended to PATH (exits 99 and prints TRIPWIRE if ever invoked) -- exit 0, no TRIPWIRE output, the resulting handoff sidecar carried provider_release_pending:true and a non-empty provider_release_next_command, plus a genuine liveness release event appended (AC1 fires regardless of provider kind). (6) Independent AC6/#291-seam corroboration (override-actor release, Task A): claimed subject 'hsession' as FLOW_AGENTS_ACTOR=canonical-x via the real CLI (actor_key stored bare as 'canonical-x', actor triple stored as claude-code:canonical-x:<host> -- deliberately differing forms); Stop as FLOW_AGENTS_ACTOR=different-y left the record status:claimed, holder, and audit_trail byte-identical (foreign actor correctly refused); Stop as FLOW_AGENTS_ACTOR=canonical-x (same actor) released it (status:released, new audit_trail entry) -- proves the actor_key-first canonical-key comparison, not a serializeActor-vs-serializeActor comparison that would have failed this exact case. (7) Independent #290 back-compat corroboration (AC2/AC6, no actor_key): hand-built a legacy-shaped assignment record with NO actor_key field (only the nested actor triple, matching pre-#291 records) for this process's own resolved actor triple, then ran Stop with no override -- record correctly transitioned to status:released via the serializeActor(existing.actor) fallback path, proving a pre-#291-shaped legacy record still releases correctly for its true holder. (8) goal_fit_hook.sh, goal_fit_escape_hatch.sh, and session_resume_roundtrip.sh: reproduced their failures (1, 5, and 13 issues respectively) as byte-identical on the pre-#292 merge-base commit after stashing every #292-changed file and rebuilding -- confirmed pre-existing, disjoint repo state, NOT part of #292's own evidence surface (session_resume_roundtrip invokes scripts/hooks/workflow-steering.js, a file untouched by this diff; goal_fit_escape_hatch and goal_fit_hook invoke the changed stop-goal-fit.js but reproduce byte-identical pre-#292 failures, confirming the #292 diff introduces no new failure in any of the three). None of these three are recorded as their own claims in this bundle -- they are disjoint, pre-existing repo state, disclosed here as prose only, never as a standalone failing/disputed claim (a failing claim would incorrectly block trust-reconcile regardless of how honestly it is annotated; per ADR 0020 the correct handling for disjoint pre-existing failures is disclosure as prose inside a passing/waived check, not a standalone fail claim). This check itself is session-local (no single reconcilable command) and is being waived as an accepted, fully-disclosed gap: none of the folded sub-checks are command-backed test_output evidence (ADR 0020 forbids waiving those), each is independently corroborated above rather than merely asserted, and the pre-existing failures they touch on are proven -- not assumed -- disjoint from AC1-AC9.

waiver.reason: Session-local summary folding non-manifest self-checks (npm run build, bare validate:source, test_trust_reconcile_manifest.sh parity self-check) plus manual/independent verifier reproductions (AC7 fail-open, AC3 github honest-disclosure, AC6/#291-seam override-actor release, AC2/#290 no-actor_key back-compat, and pre-existing-failure disjointness for goal_fit_hook/goal_fit_escape_hatch/session_resume_roundtrip) that have no single CI-manifest-reconcilable command by construction. ADR 0020 forbids waiving any command-backed (test_output) check; none of the folded sub-checks are command-backed, and each is independently corroborated (not merely attested) in this same summary and in the verification report. The pre-existing goal_fit_hook/goal_fit_escape_hatch/session_resume_roundtrip disjointness is disclosed here as prose only -- deliberately NOT as a standalone claim, since a standalone disputed/failing claim blocks trust-reconcile regardless of honesty (session-local classification is not a pass bypass) and disjoint pre-existing repo state is not part of #292's own evidence surface.

claims.fieldOrBehavior: A Stop event on a session whose state.json.status is NOT in the LIVENESS_TERMINAL set ({delivered, accepted, archived}, imported not re-derived) appends a liveness release event for that session's (subjectId=slug, actor=branchActorKey) pair.

When the effective assignment provider is local-file and a "claimed" record exists for the session's subject_id held by this actor, the Stop hook's non-terminal path releases it (status becomes "released") and a subsequent assignment-provider status read reports effective_state: "free".

When the effective assignment provider is github, the Stop hook never invokes gh and never calls render-release; it writes handoff.json.provider_release_pending: true plus a next_steps entry naming the concrete follow-up command.

After a non-terminal Stop release, handoff.json's summary, next_steps, and release-outcome fields reflect the session's actual last state.json status/phase, never stale/empty values from an earlier phase transition.

A Stop event on a session already at a terminal status (delivered/accepted/archived) does not emit a second liveness release event and does not error on a second local-file release attempt (tolerated no-op).
The release targets only the current actor's own held claim; a fixture where a different actor holds the local-file claim for the session's subject_id must not be released by this actor's Stop hook.

A Stop event with a missing build/ directory, a corrupt assignment record, an unresolved actor, or a missing/malformed assignment-provider-settings.json never throws and never changes the Stop hook's own exit code/gating behavior for unrelated goal-fit warnings.

LIVENESS_TERMINAL is exported once from src/cli/workflow-sidecar.ts and imported (never re-declared) by the Stop hook's release logic, so the terminal/non-terminal boundary can never drift between the two files.
The new eval is registered in evals/run.sh (integration block), evals/ci/run-baseline.sh (CHECKS array), and .github/workflows/ci.yml (a --check step), per ADR 0020's manifest==ci parity rule.
